name: X-Force: Non-Servers Communicating with External IP Classified as Dynamic
notes:
This rule triggers when a local host, not identified as a server, communicates with an IP that is considered to be dynamic. Usually there is no reason for internal hosts to be communicating with dynamic ranges.

Note: The Confidence Factor ranges between 0 to 100. On assets of lower importance, an administrator might weigh an X-Force rule to trigger on a higher confidence factor of 75. When tuning rules, customers should think of a scale where 50 is the tipping point.

See the blog https://ibm.biz/BdPEXv for details.
conditions_text: when a flow or an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Servers
when the context is Local to Remote, Remote to Remote
when Destination IP is categorized by X-Force as Dynamic IPs with confidence value greater than 75
TD_list: APPLY X-Force: Non-Servers Communicating with External IP Classified as Dynamic on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when a flow or an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Servers
AND when the context is Local to Remote, Remote to Remote
AND when Destination IP is categorized by X-Force as Dynamic IPs with confidence value greater than 75
RED_list: Event name: Non-Servers Communicating with External IP Classified as Dynamic

Event description: This rule will notify when an internal host that is not a server, communicates with an IP that is considered to be dynamic. Usually there is no reason for internal hosts to be communicating with dynamic ranges.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
